title: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
subhead: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

title: US and Iran escalate 'double blockade' of Strait of Hormuz
Zelensky condemns Russia's deadly strikes in Ukraine as Moscow seeks peace for May 9 parade
Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Mohammadi 'between life and death,' her lawyer says
subhead: Narges Mohammadi had two suspected heart attacks on March 24 and May 1 in prison in northern Iran, according to her supporters.

title: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
subhead: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

title: 'Vincent Bolloré's strategy is to take over the media and publishers to turn them into propaganda tools'
subhead: Sociologist Gisèle Sapiro rejects the idea that the dismissal of Grasset publisher Olivier Nora is a mere 'epiphenomena in the history of financial capitalism' and stresses the political and ideological threat that this decision poses.

title: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
subhead: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

The 'alpha male' holds little appeal for women (and comes at a high cost)
subhead: While many young men are receptive to the hyper-masculine rhetoric that is spread across social media, the stereotypes touted as ways to attract women rarely succeed, writes columnist Maïa Mazaurette. Instead, she encourages men to take a genuine interest in women.

title: Putin's waning popularity worries Kremlin five months ahead of Russian parliamentary elections
subhead: Internet shutdowns have hastened the decline in public trust in the president, amid economic hardships that have affected citizens' daily lives and the war in Ukraine that has only kept dragging on.

title: French Interior Minister Nuñez: 'I have no problem with Islam in France. But I fight those who use it to undermine our Republic'
subhead: Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez shares his view on the relationship between Islam and the French Republic. He reveals the contents of a bill he is presenting, aimed at combating 'separatism' and 'entryism.'
